text: Mild non-proliferative diabetic retinopathy: microaneurysms may be present. Annual screening is recommended.
Moderate NPDR: increased microaneurysms, hemorrhages. Consider closer follow-up every 6–12 months.
Severe NPDR: extensive hemorrhages and venous beading. High risk of progression.
PDR: neovascularization present. Immediate treatment such as laser or anti-VEGF is recommended.
